assessment.approach: The challenge requires a two-step interaction: first, POST to /api/lattice/seed to obtain a runId and a capsule. Second, PUT to /api/lattice/unseal using the runId in the body and the capsule (base64url encoded and prefixed with 'Bearer ') in the Authorization header.
evidenceUsed: Relation: POST response $.runId to PUT request $.runId
Relation: POST response $.capsule to PUT request Authorization header (base64url-encoded + 'Bearer ' prefix)
uncertainties: None, the workflow is explicitly defined by the evidence relations.

import { test } from 'node:test'; import assert from 'node:assert/strict'; test('Lattice Capsule Challenge', async () => { const origin = 'http://127.0.0.1:64356'; const headers = { 'x-gym-benchmark-run': 'gym-medium-features-bdf51557', 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }; const seedRes = await fetch(`${origin}/api/lattice/seed`, { method: 'POST', headers, body: JSON.stringify({ label: 'medium-test' }) }); assert.strictEqual(seedRes.status, 200); const { runId, capsule } = await seedRes.json(); const authHeader = 'Bearer ' + Buffer.from(capsule).toString('base64url'); const unsealRes = await fetch(`${origin}/api/lattice/unseal`, { method: 'PUT', headers: { ...headers, 'Authorization': authHeader }, body: JSON.stringify({ runId }) }); assert.strictEqual(unsealRes.status, 200); const result = await unsealRes.json(); assert.strictEqual(result.accepted, true); assert.strictEqual(result.challenge, 'medium'); });
